Basic Idioms & Expressions.meaning: A friendly way to ask 'how are you?'.
A way to say goodbye and wish someone well.
Basic Idioms & Expressions.example: It was great to see you! Take care.

Basic Idioms & Expressions.meaning: A common way to say goodbye.
Basic Idioms & Expressions.example: I have to leave now, see you later!

Basic Idioms & Expressions.meaning: Used to show agreement or approval of a suggestion.
Used to say that something is okay or that you are happy to help.
Used to say that you have no preference.
Basic Idioms & Expressions.example: - 'Do you want tea or coffee?' - 'I don't mind.'

Basic Idioms & Expressions.meaning: Moving fast because you have little time.
Basic Idioms & Expressions.example: I'm in a hurry, I can't talk right now.

Basic Idioms & Expressions.meaning: Something that is very easy to do.
Basic Idioms & Expressions.example: The test was a piece of cake.
Good luck with your job interview tomorrow!

Basic Idioms & Expressions.meaning: Not late; at the correct scheduled time.
Basic Idioms & Expressions.example: The train arrived exactly on time.
I go to the beach from time to time.
We eat out at a restaurant once in a while.
